summary: Media player crashes (native SIGSEGV) when switching playback to a sink that needs sample-rate conversion
description: On the AAOS head unit, com.autolinq.mediaplayer terminates with a native crash the moment playback is routed to an output device whose sample rate differs from the decoded stream (e.g. plugging in the USB DAC at 44.1 kHz while the track is 48 kHz, or switching to the Bluetooth A2DP sink). The app's native audio engine (libmediaengine.so) faults with signal 11 (SIGSEGV) on the real-time callback thread 'mediaengine-cb'. The tombstone points at the internal sample-rate converter write path: MultiChannelResampler::writeFrame, called from SampleRateConverter::onProcess while the flowgraph is pulling data. fault addr is near-null (0x10), which looks like a null / empty input frame being pushed into the resampler before the graph is fully configured. Audio drops out and the media surface goes black. 100% reproducible on rate-mismatched routes; no repro when the source and sink rates already match. See attached tombstone.
